title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: For many years, it was (0) widely believed that learning a second language as a child might cause confusion. However, modern neuroscience has (1) this myth, proving that bilingualism actually provides significant cognitive advantages. Researchers have found that the effort required to (2) between two different linguistic systems strengthens the brain's executive function. This mental flexibility (3) bilingual individuals to filter out distractions and focus more effectively on complex tasks. Furthermore, there is evidence to suggest that being fluent in more than one language can (4) the onset of age-related cognitive decline. This is because the constant mental exercise (5) in managing two languages builds a 'cognitive reserve' that protects the brain. In social terms, bilingualism also (6) a deeper understanding of different cultures, as language is often the key to unlocking unique worldviews. While the process of achieving fluency can be (7) and requires consistent practice, the long-term rewards are immense. From improved problem-solving skills to enhanced empathy, the benefits of a second language (8) far beyond mere communication, shaping the way we perceive and interact with the world around us in increasingly profound ways.

example.explanation: “Widely believed” is a standard collocation used to describe a common public opinion.

questions.explanation: “Debunked” is the specific verb for proving that a popular myth or belief is false.
“Switch between” is the standard term for changing from one system or language to another.
“Enables someone to [verb]” is a formal way to say something makes an action possible.
“Delay the onset” is a common medical/scientific collocation for slowing the start of a condition.
“Involved in” is the correct past participle for describing an activity that is part of a process.
“Fosters” means to encourage the development of a quality or feeling.
“Demanding” is the most appropriate adjective for a process that requires a lot of effort.
“Extend far beyond” is a common phrase meaning to have a much wider reach or impact.
